justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified as a domain model change via BR-2 and the bounded_context section calling out anti-corruption layer concerns; (2) Data ownership clearly raised — StockLevel owned by Warehouse, 'Stock Availability Projection' marked as NOT YET DEFINED, and BR-2 enforcing that Sales must not directly access Warehouse aggregates; (3) TOCTOU race condition thoroughly identified in BR-3 ('point-in-time snapshot, NOT a guarantee'), edge case #1 (simultaneous orders exceeding stock), and escalation #5 (concurrency model undefined); (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction explicitly surfaced — 'Stock Reservation' listed as NOT YET DEFINED in ubiquitous language, BR-4 introduces reservation TTL requirements, escalation #5 proposes hybrid soft-check/hard-reservation; (5) Architectural coupling escalated across five detailed escalations covering integration pattern, resilience, concurrency, and partial fulfillment — while MJ is not named explicitly, the escalations are structured as handoffs to an architect; (6) New cross-context invariants stated in BR-1 (stock >= 0), BR-2 (no cross-context aggregate access), BR-3 (check ≠ guarantee).

observations.text: Exceptional ubiquitous language section — marks Stock Reservation and Stock Availability Projection as NOT YET DEFINED, and flags 'Blocked Order' as AMBIGUOUS, which is exactly the domain authority behavior expected from Bird

BR-3 perfectly articulates the TOCTOU problem: 'Between the check and the actual pick, stock may change due to concurrent orders, damage, shrinkage, or corrections' — this is textbook domain-driven risk identification

Edge cases are outstanding — covers simultaneous orders, post-confirmation shortfall, inter-event stock arrival, partial fulfillment, warehouse unavailability, and never-stocked products, all marked UNDEFINED where appropriate
Confidence level of 35 with clearly articulated high/low confidence areas and explicit assumptions demonstrates intellectual honesty and appropriate domain authority restraint
Rejection reasons section adds value by flagging that any implementation directly coupling the contexts or treating eventually-consistent data as real-time would violate stated business rules
Escalation #5 proposes a hybrid soft-check/hard-reservation pattern as recommendation, showing Bird can suggest domain-appropriate solutions while still deferring the architectural decision
Escalations do not explicitly name MJ as the recipient for architectural coupling decisions — the handoff target is implicit rather than explicit, though the content is architecturally complete
